Where are the market levels as Warsh begins his press conference
Fed Chair Warsh: We recognize that inflation has been running "well ahead" of 2%
Markets shift in a hawkish direction after the FOMC statement and dot plot
The full FOMC statement from the June 2026 meeting.

FOMC preview: Why this is one for the headline traders
Fed's Chair Warsh: I don't want to prejudge the outcomes of the communications review.
Fed's Chair Warsh: For me, submitting a dot is not helpful in the conduct of policy.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The level of Fed restriction is uneven.
Fed's Chair Warsh: I am pretty open minded.
Fed's Chair Warsh: I expect by year-end there will be a broad review of communications, press conferences, dots, and meetings.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Policymakers don't feel bound by their dots. I did not hear tons of conviction on the projection submissions.
Fed's Chair Warsh noted that dot plot submissions came in with pencils that have big erasers.
Fed's Chair Warsh asked about the future of the dot plot: We've got a task for that.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Fed policy appears to be restrictive vis-à-vis the housing market, but not financial markets.
Fed's Chair Warsh, asked about the future of policy: We've dropped forward guidance.
Fed's Chair Warsh: We have the capability and the commitment to deliver 2% inflation.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The 2% goal will be outside the scope of the inflation task force.
Fed's Chair Warsh: I see no reason until we have delivered the 2% goal, to revisit that goal.
Fed's Chair Warsh: 2% inflation is the Fed's longheld objective.
Fed's Chair Warsh: I expect to see some results by fall, the conclusion of task forces by the year-end.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Task forces to begin work in the next couple of weeks.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The task force on inflation frameworks will examine drivers of inflation.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The task force on productivity and jobs will survey the reach of AI and other general-purpose tech.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The data task force will consider new data sources and methodological changes.
Fed's Chair Warsh: I expect the communications task force to recommend an SEP change.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The balance sheet task force will review the benefits and risks of the ample reserves regime.
Fed's Chair Warsh: I expect to propose changes, including to the summary of projections.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Task forces will ultimately propose next steps.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Task forces include inflation frameworks.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Task forces will be supported by Fed staff.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Each independent task force will have the best minds inside and outside the economics profession.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Task forces include data sources, productivity, and jobs.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Communications, balance sheet, data sources, productivity and jobs, and the Fed's inflation frameworks will be studied.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The task force on communications, balance sheet, use of data sources.
Fed's Chair Warsh: I am appointing a task force in 5 areas.
Fed's Chair Warsh: I refrained from submitting projections of my own.
Fed's Chair Warsh: In the statement, also absent is forward guidance, which is not well-suited to the current policy state.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Today's policy statement was shorter and simpler. The statement dispenses with older language.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Timely opportunity to review practices, reaffirm mission when leadership changes.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The FOMC members are unanimous that they will deliver price stability.
Fed's Chair Warsh: The recent past need not be a prologue to inflation.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Persistently high prices are a burden.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Economic activity expanding at a solid pace.
Fed's Chair Warsh: Inflation is well ahead of the 2% goal.
